seoTitle: Ravenswood vs Red Gully — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Ravenswood and Red Gully. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Ravenswood vs Red Gully: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Ravenswood and Red Gully.
heading: Ravenswood vs Red Gully — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ravenswood, Western Australia and Red Gully, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Red Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Ravenswood (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ravenswood or Red Gully safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ravenswood has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Red Gully scores 44/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ravenswood scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Red Gully scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ravenswood or Red Gully?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Red Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
